chunks.text: The afternoon library held a quiet atmosphere.
I picked up a small essay collection and opened the first chapter.
The author gently described small discoveries in daily life.
In one paragraph he expressed a memory
in another he openly described the desire of his young days.
The writing strongly attracted my heart and appealed to me.
The style was as calm as a philosophy book, yet something in it inspired me.
I glanced out of the window, then returned to the page.
The mind that composed these sentences was quiet, but it was full of passion.
Outside, a small child ran past.
I watched him with a curious feeling.
Today, I preferred this one book to anything else.
